description_long: This object is a vibrant pink water lily flower with multiple layers of broad, pointed petals radiating outward from a central cluster of white, slender stamens. The petals are arranged in a whorled, overlapping pattern, creating a full and lush appearance. The outermost petals are larger and more widely spaced, while the inner petals are smaller and more tightly packed around the center. The flower lacks a visible stem or leaves, and the base is slightly concave, giving it a floating, delicate look. The surface of the petals appears soft and matte, with subtle color gradients from deep magenta to lighter pink. This type of flower is commonly found in urban parks, ponds, or decorative water features.
description: A lush pink water lily flower with layered, pointed petals and a cluster of white stamens at its center. The petals are arranged in a whorled, overlapping pattern, giving the flower a full and vibrant appearance.
description_view_0: The flower is seen from a slightly elevated angle, highlighting the layered arrangement of the petals and the prominent white stamens at the center.
description_view_1: This view shows the flower from a different side, revealing the asymmetry in petal spacing and the depth of the overlapping layers.
description_view_2: The flower is viewed from a lower angle, emphasizing the concave base and the dense cluster of stamens surrounded by tightly packed petals.
description_view_3: This angle displays the broad spread of the outer petals and the subtle color variations across the petal surfaces.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a whorled, radial configuration with multiple overlapping layers. Each layer of petals is symmetrically spaced around the central axis, and the petals overlap at their bases, creating a dense, full appearance typical of water lilies.
